title: Best IDX Websites for Real Estate Agents in 2026 (Tested for Lead Conversion, Not Just Looks)
description: Real Geeks, Sierra Interactive, kvCORE, Placester, IDX Broker — we built five demo sites and tracked which ones actually converted searchers into leads. Here's what we found.
tldr: Sierra Interactive wins for serious agents and small teams who want maximum lead conversion ($499/mo). Real Geeks is the best price-to-performance pick at $299/mo. Placester is the only credible budget option ($99/mo) — but expect to grow out of it within a year. Skip free MLS-bundled sites; they don't capture leads.

body.text: An IDX website does one job: take a stranger searching homes and turn them into a registered lead in your CRM. Every other feature — design polish, blog, neighborhood pages — is in service of that one conversion. We built demo sites on five platforms, ran identical Facebook Ad traffic to each, and measured registration rate, time-on-site, and 30-day return rate. Here's what came out clean.
Each platform got a 4-week test in the same metro market with identical ad budget ($500 in Facebook Lead Ads + $500 in Google search ads). We measured:

items: Registration rate — % of visitors who became a lead
Time-to-first-action — how long until they searched, saved, or registered
Mobile bounce rate (78% of traffic was mobile)
30-day return rate — % of leads who came back without us prompting
Lead-to-CRM speed — how fast did the lead show up in our follow-up tool

body.name: Sierra Interactive
pros: Highest registration rate in our test — 7.1% of visitors became leads
Mobile speed is best-in-class (Lighthouse 92+)
Built-in CRM is actually usable (not bolted on)
Forced-registration after 3 searches drives lead capture without killing UX
Excellent SEO foundation — neighborhood pages rank without much help
cons: Setup fee + monthly puts year one near $6,500
Customization requires their support team — limited DIY editing
Aesthetic is functional, not stunning — looks like 2020 not 2026

body.text: Sierra Interactive is what serious agents quietly buy. It's not flashy and the demo isn't pretty, but every design decision is in service of converting search traffic into registered leads. The forced-registration prompt after 3 searches sounds aggressive — until you see the numbers. Sierra's 7.1% conversion rate was double the worst performer in our test.

body.name: Real Geeks
pros: 5.4% conversion rate — second only to Sierra
Bundled CRM and drip nurture (saves $80/mo vs. standalone CRM)
Done-for-you Facebook Ads template that converts at $4-7 cost-per-lead
Solid mobile experience and acceptable Lighthouse scores
Setup is faster than Sierra (2-3 days to live site)
cons: Templates feel a bit dated — limited design flexibility
Customer support is slower than Sierra's during peak hours
Add-on costs (premium IDX, additional users) stack up over time

body.text: Real Geeks is the right pick if you want most of Sierra's conversion power at 60% of the price. The bundled drip nurture is the killer feature — leads who don't convert in week one get a 14-touch sequence automatically. Most platforms charge extra for this; Real Geeks builds it in.
3. kvCORE Websites — Solid if you're already in the ecosystem

body.name: kvCORE (Inside Real Estate)
pros: Strong IDX search and map functionality
AI-powered behavioral nurture — ranks leads by activity
Built for brokerages that need agent recruitment + retention
Compliance and document management built-in
cons: 4.2% registration rate — meaningfully behind Sierra and Real Geeks
UI is heavy; the back-end has a lot of clicks for simple tasks
Locked into Inside Real Estate ecosystem (CRM, lead routing, etc.)

body.text: 4. Placester — The budget pick that's good enough

body.name: Placester
pros: By far the cheapest credible option in the category
Templates are surprisingly modern (better aesthetic than Sierra)
Easy DIY editing — no support ticket required for basic changes
Decent mobile experience
cons: 3.1% registration rate — meaningfully lower than the leaders
No built-in CRM — you'll need Follow Up Boss or similar separately
IDX search is functional but slower than Sierra/Real Geeks
Lead-to-CRM handoff requires a Zapier integration

body.text: Placester is the right call if you're new to the business, can't afford $300+/mo, and need a real website right now. You'll likely outgrow it within 12-18 months as your lead volume scales — but spending $99/mo for a year is a lot smarter than spending $500/mo for a year while you're still building your sphere.
5. IDX Broker — For DIY agents who already have a website

body.name: IDX Broker
pros: Cheapest way to add MLS search to an existing WordPress/Squarespace site
Works with any CMS or framework via embed code
Strong technical foundation (search speed, MLS data freshness)
Good for agents who already invested in a custom-designed site
cons: Not a full website — just the IDX search tool
No CRM, no drip, no Facebook Ad integration
Setup requires technical comfort or a developer

items: Top-25% producer focused on lead conversion → Sierra Interactive. The 7.1% conversion rate justifies the price.
Solo agent or small team wanting bundled value → Real Geeks. Best price-to-performance.
Brokerage or large team in the Inside Real Estate ecosystem → kvCORE.
New agent on tight budget → Placester. Plan to upgrade within 18 months.
DIY agent with an existing custom site → IDX Broker for the search tool only.

body.title: Hidden cost reality
body.text: All five platforms have setup fees, MLS feed fees, or per-user upgrades that aren't on the public pricing page. Budget 25-35% above sticker for year one (setup + MLS data + add-ons). Get the full quote before you sign.

items: Free MLS-bundled agent sites — zero lead capture, zero customization
Squarespace + manual MLS embed — slow, breaks every MLS feed update
Wix's 'Real Estate' template — looks fine, doesn't convert (1.8% in our test)
Custom WordPress builds with no IDX — fine for branding, useless for lead gen
